rulings.Case Title: KATIUSKA ABURTO et al VS. ANNA T WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 22, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS ANNA WONG, LARRY WONG, JACK WONG DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer to (unnumbered) "15th" cause of action for conspiracy is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Cause of action uncertain because all causes of action are alleged against all defendants. Defendant Larry Wong's summary adjudication motion was granted as to the 1st, 2nd, 5th, 8th, 9th and 10th causes of action and may not be revived by alleging conspiracy. Plaintiffs must allege facts that show the formation and operation of a conspiracy, wrongful acts of the conspirators thereto, and damage resulting therefrom. Facts must be alleged with enough clarity to put defendants on notice as to what causes of action they allegedly conspired to commit. A party objecting to a complaint may demurrer and answer at the same time. CCP section 430.30(c). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR RABINOVICH VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DBA FIRST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 22, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN LOANSTAR TRUSTEE SERVICES LLC MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted without leaave to amend. Judgment was entered in defendant's favor on January 12, 2009, based on November 26, 2009 order sustaining a demurrer without leave to amend. Court lacks jurisdiction to set aside or amend the judgment which is the final determination of the rights of the parties. CCP section 577. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. CLIFT HOLDINGS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 22, 2010, line 17, DEFENDANT MORGANS HOTEL GROUP CO., CLIFT HOLDINGS, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Defendants fail to address plaintiff's causes of action for negligence, premises liability, and invasion of privacy. Defendant has presented no responses to specifically targeted discovery allowing an inference that plaintiff neither possesses, nor reasonbly can obtain, evidence to support her causes of action. Since burden has not shifted, court need not rule on parties evidentiary objections. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEEANNA M. BROSSOIT VS. DREXEL BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Drexel Bradshaw And Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Motion To Strike Complaint And Portions Of Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 22, 2010, line 10, Defendants Drexel Bradshaw and Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Motion to Strike Complaint and Portions of Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch). Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must comply with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.110, 2.111, and 2.112(4). Plaintiff must plead the basis for an award of attorney fees and delete arguments and citations to case law. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEEANNA M. BROSSOIT VS. DREXEL BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Drexel Bradshaw And Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Demurrer To Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 22, 2010, line 10, Defendants Drexel Bradshaw and Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Demurrer To Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch). Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to 1st cause of action, plaintiff must allege causation and plead around the unclean hands defense. As to the 2nd cause of action, plaintiff must particularly allege defendant's knowledge of falsity and damages. As to the 3rd cause of action, plaintiff must specify why defendants had no reasonable grounds to believe his representations were true and damages. As to the 4th cause of action, plaintiff must allege facts regarding the breach of contract terms and damages, and plead around the defense of plaintiff's non-performance. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JACK SHAW VS. CATHY KOHLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgemnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 22, 2010, line 14, DEFENDANT CATHY KOHLER Motion To Set Aside Default and Default Judgment. Motion granted. Defendant establishes mistake, surprise or excusable neglect and acted quickly to set aside the default. The law favors adjudication on the merits. Defendant must file her answer on or before February 23, 2010. =(302/CWW)
